top_signals.title: ECB’s Escriva Says Energy Disruption Will Persist Despite Deal
top_signals.summary: European Central Bank Governing Council member Jose Luis Escriva warned that complications over energy supplies are likely to persist, despite an agreement to re-open the Strait of Hormuz.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: ‘Philanthropy isn’t an act of kindness, it is a power’ — collector Anastasia Bukhman
top_signals.summary: Following its donation to the National Portrait Gallery last year, the Bukhman Foundation is seeking to nurture a new generation of curators
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Who will pay for Canada’s proposed west coast pipeline?
top_signals.summary: The project aims to send up to 1mn barrels a day to Asia and reduce reliance on the US
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: SpaceX gains 5% in premarket trading after 20% spike on Monday
top_signals.summary: CEO Elon Musk said Sunday that the company "might be able to reach approximately" $1 trillion revenue in 2030."
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Robinhood cuts 10% of workforce as Tenev touts business strength
top_signals.summary: Robinhood cuts about 10% of staff as CEO Vlad Tenev says business “has never been stronger” despite weak Q1 trading.

top_signals.title: UK to ban social media access for children under 16
top_signals.summary: The ban will apply to all “user-to-user platforms, whose purpose is to enable social interaction and which allow users to post material, alongside algorithms,” according to a press release from the government’s Department for Science, Innovation and Technology.
